doom.driver: Iran's Revolutionary Guard on Wednesday threatened to extend the Middle East conflict "beyond the region" if the U.S. and Israel resume attacks against Tehran.
doom.blurb: IRAN THREATENS TO EXPORT THE WAR
doom.summary: Just days after Trump 'postponed' a scheduled Iran strike like a dentist appointment, Iran's Revolutionary Guard is now threatening to take the conflict 'beyond the region' — which is diplomatic speak for 'we have friends and missiles.' Meanwhile, Putin landed in Beijing hours after Trump left, Xi is hosting everyone like a geopolitical Airbnb, and the Power of Siberia 2 pipeline is suddenly back on the agenda. The axis of 'not America' is having a very productive week.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Iran threatening regional escalation while Putin-Xi summit revives energy partnerships and Chinese supertankers quietly exit the Strait of Hormuz — three simultaneous pressure points.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: CISA admin leaked AWS GovCloud keys on GitHub and secret credentials sat exposed since November 2025 — the US cybersecurity agency has a cybersecurity problem.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Google I/O unveiled Gemini 3.5 Flash and DeepMind's Hassabis invoked the 'foothills of the singularity' — either inspiring or the most ominous keynote closer in tech history.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Andrej Karpathy joining Anthropic signals top talent is consolidating at safety-focused labs, which is directionally good but underscores how fast the capability race is moving.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Ebola case count hit 600 suspected cases and 139 deaths as WHO warns numbers will rise further, while the US cuts public health funding and criticizes the WHO's response speed.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — wide aerial view angled down at 45 degrees, camera looking steeply down at a vast symbolic scene rendered in deep navy shadow and smoldering orange: a colossal stone chessboard stretching across a scorched desert plateau, the arid ochre terrain of the Iranian highland visible at the edges where the board's borders dissolve into cracked earth and pale dust, the Alborz mountain range's white-capped silhouette ghosting along the upper horizon beneath a smoggy amber sky — the board itself is mid-collapse, its center squares fracturing and tilting downward into a dark void below, fissures radiating outward from the middle like cracks in ancient stone, deep navy shadow pooling in the gaps — chess pieces rendered as squat chunky 16-bit figures scatter and tumble at the collapse point, some already fallen into the darkness, others teetering at the crumbling rim, the pieces extending in unbroken rows far beyond the board's broken center toward every edge of the frame, implying a conflict spilling outward in all directions — smoldering orange embers glow from deep within the fissures below, casting dramatic upward light against the underside of the tilting stone squares, warmth feeling deeply threatening — heavy shadows dominate three-quarters of the frame, the collapsing center the only source of light.

dawn.driver: Breakthrough drug reverses aging in skin and dramatically speeds healing. Scientists have discovered that a topical anti-aging drug called ABT-263 can dramatically improve wound healing in older skin. The treatment works by removing damaged 'senescent' cells that accumulate
dawn.blurb: SCIENTISTS FOUND THE SWITCH THAT REVERSES SKIN AGING
dawn.summary: In a week heavy with Ebola escalations and geopolitical friction, a quiet but profound discovery landed in the labs: a topical drug that clears out the body's 'zombie cells' — the senescent cells that accumulate with age and slow healing — dramatically restoring wound repair in older skin. This isn't cosmetic vanity science; it points toward a genuine mechanism for reversing age-related biological decline. The same cellular pathway implicated here underlies chronic inflammation across the body, meaning the implications stretch far beyond skin.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Senolytic therapy targeting aging cells is one of the most promising fronts in longevity medicine, and a working topical drug marks a meaningful translational step from lab to clinic.

factors.name: Medicine
factors.detail: Faster wound healing in elderly patients could prevent millions of deaths and amputations annually from chronic wounds — a largely invisible epidemic in aging populations.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: A gray wolf was spotted in Sequoia National Park for the first time in over 100 years, a small but stirring signal that nature's resilience can outpace our damage.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Wind-assisted cargo ships could cut shipping fuel use by more than half if routes are optimized for prevailing winds — a scalable, low-tech answer to one of shipping's hardest emissions problems.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: 'Taiwan Travelogue' won the 2026 International Booker Prize — the first Mandarin-language work ever to do so — a reminder that literature still crosses the borders that politics can't.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. A fog-drenched nursing home common room seen from mid-distance through a rain-streaked window, the glass itself dominating the foreground, fat amber droplets running down the pane in slow rivers, the scene inside filtered soft and impressionistic through the wet haze. Deep golden amber light pools from a single floor lamp inside, warm teal shadows filling the corners and the fog-blurred corridor beyond. An elderly person's forearm rests palm-up on a blanket draped over a wheelchair armrest, the skin rendered in translucent pixel layers — fine creases, faint sun spots — and at the wrist a small glowing patch of faintly warmer pink-gold pixels, barely perceptible, a wound site beginning to close. On a side table beside them a tiny dropper bottle catches the amber lamplight, its pale gold liquid a single bright pixel cluster against the cool teal shadows. Outside the rain-blurred window the street is soft grey dissolution, headlights smearing into long golden streaks through the downpour. The mood is intimate and radiant, tender quietness inside, weather-erased world outside, the healing happening in stillness while the storm presses against the glass.
